name: Tomato
description: One of the most popular vegetables in Mauritius. Thrives in warm weather and produces abundantly from winter to summer.
care_notes: Water regularly at the base. Stake plants when tall. Watch for whitefly in summer.

name: Brinjal (Eggplant)
description: A warm-season crop that thrives in the Mauritius summer heat. Popular in local cuisine.
care_notes: Needs warm soil. Mulch to retain moisture. Protect young plants from strong winds.

name: Chili Pepper
description: A Mauritius staple in every household garden. Grows well year-round in warm conditions.
care_notes: Drought tolerant once established. Harvest regularly to encourage more fruiting.

name: Cucumber
description: Fast-growing vine that loves the Mauritius summer heat. Requires a trellis or support.
care_notes: Water frequently and consistently. Provide support structure. Harvest before over-ripening.

name: Bitter Gourd
description: Extremely popular in Mauritian cuisine. Thrives in tropical heat on a trellis or fence.
care_notes: Needs strong trellis. Soak seeds 24 hours before planting. Harvest while still green.

name: Ridge Gourd
description: Popular local vegetable grown on trellises. Also used as loofah when dried.
care_notes: Needs strong support. Regular watering required. Harvest young for best flavor.

name: Pumpkin
description: Space-demanding but highly productive. Grows well in most Mauritius regions.
care_notes: Needs plenty of space. Keep soil moist when fruiting. Cure harvested pumpkins in sun.

name: Cabbage
description: A cool-season crop best grown during Mauritius winter in the central plateau or south.
care_notes: Needs cool temperatures. Protect from caterpillars. Use row covers if needed.

name: Lettuce
description: Fast-growing cool-season green. Best grown in Mauritius winter, especially in higher regions.
care_notes: Keep soil moist. Harvest outer leaves to extend the season. Bolts in heat.

name: Spinach
description: Nutritious cool-season green. Grows quickly in Mauritius winter months.
care_notes: Sow directly. Keep evenly moist. Cut leaves 1cm above ground for regrowth.

name: French Beans
description: Quick-growing legume that fixes nitrogen in the soil. Very popular in Mauritius.
care_notes: Direct sow. Keep soil moist. Harvest when pods are young and crisp.

name: Long Beans (Gros Pois)
description: Heat-tolerant bean that thrives in Mauritius summer. Needs trellis. Very productive.
care_notes: Provide trellis. Drought tolerant once established. Harvest daily when pods form.

name: Okra (Lalo)
description: A Mauritius staple. Extremely heat-tolerant and produces abundantly in summer.
care_notes: Soak seeds overnight before planting. Harvest every 2-3 days when pods are 7-10cm.

name: Sweet Potato (Patate Douce)
description: Easy-to-grow root vegetable that spreads as ground cover. Very low maintenance.
care_notes: Plant slips (cuttings) in well-drained soil. Little fertiliser needed. Harvest before rains.

name: Spring Onion
description: Fast-growing essential herb/vegetable in Mauritius cooking. Grows in both seasons.
care_notes: Can be grown in containers. Keep moist. Harvest by cutting, leaving roots to regrow.

name: Radish
description: One of the fastest vegetables to grow. Ready in just 3-4 weeks. Great for beginners.
care_notes: Direct sow. Keep soil moist for even germination. Harvest promptly when ready.

name: Corn (Maize)
description: Tall, space-efficient when grown in rows. Part of the Three Sisters companion planting group.
care_notes: Plant in blocks of at least 4x4 for wind pollination. Mound soil around base when 30cm tall.

name: Basil
description: Essential herb in Mauritian and Mediterranean cooking. Repels pests when grown near tomatoes.
care_notes: Pinch flowers to extend leaf production. Water at base, not leaves. Harvest regularly.

name: Coriander (Dhania)
description: Cornerstone of Mauritian cuisine. Best grown in cooler months in shaded spots.
care_notes: Sow in stages every 2-3 weeks. Bolts quickly in heat. Harvest leaves before flowering.

name: Mint
description: Vigorous perennial herb. Grow in containers to control spreading. Used widely in Mauritian cooking.
care_notes: Grow in containers to prevent invasive spreading. Keep moist. Harvest regularly.

name: Thyme
description: Drought-tolerant perennial herb. Excellent companion plant and insect repellent.
care_notes: Well-drained soil essential. Drought tolerant. Trim regularly to prevent woody stems.

name: Rosemary
description: Mediterranean herb that adapts well to Mauritius dry/warm coastal areas.
care_notes: Avoid overwatering — roots rot easily. Prune after flowering to keep bushy.

name: Parsley
description: Slow to germinate but very productive biennial. Best in Mauritius winter.
care_notes: Soak seeds 24 hours before planting. Slow germination — be patient. Harvest outer stems first.

name: Lemongrass
description: Tropical grass herb used widely in Mauritian teas and cooking. Repels mosquitoes.
care_notes: Divide clumps every 2-3 years. Drought tolerant once established. Harvest outer stalks.

name: Chives
description: Mild onion-flavored herb. Easy to grow in pots or borders. Deters aphids.
care_notes: Cut back to 2cm above ground after harvesting. Divide clumps every 3 years.

name: Papaya
description: Very fast-growing tropical fruit tree. Can produce fruit within 7-9 months from seed.
care_notes: Protect from strong winds. Never overwater — roots rot easily. Plant multiple for pollination.

name: Pineapple
description: Mauritius pineapples are world-famous. Plant crowns or slips. Takes 18 months to first harvest.
care_notes: Plant crowns or side shoots. Very drought tolerant. Acidic soil preferred.

name: Passion Fruit
description: Vigorous climbing vine producing delicious fruit. Needs a strong trellis or fence.
care_notes: Needs strong support structure. Prune after fruiting. Plant in sheltered spot.

name: Watermelon
description: Space-hungry but rewarding. Best grown in the warm north and west of Mauritius.
care_notes: Needs very warm soil. Reduce watering when fruit develops. Thump to check ripeness.

name: Strawberry
description: Grows well in Mauritius winter in the cooler central and southern regions.
care_notes: Protect fruit from slugs. Remove runners to encourage fruiting. Replace plants every 3 years.

name: Marigold
description: The ultimate companion plant. Repels nematodes, aphids and whitefly. Grows anywhere.
care_notes: Low maintenance. Deadhead regularly to extend flowering. Grows in almost any soil.

name: Sunflower
description: Fast-growing annual that attracts pollinators. Can be grown as trellis for climbing plants.
care_notes: Direct sow. Stake in windy areas. Harvest seeds when back of head turns brown.

name: Zinnia
description: Colorful, heat-loving annual. Excellent pollinator attractant. Easy for beginners.
care_notes: Direct sow. Drought tolerant once established. Deadhead to encourage continuous blooming.

name: Bougainvillea
description: Iconic Mauritius ornamental climber. Almost no care needed once established.
care_notes: Drought stress triggers blooming. Reduce watering to encourage flowers. Prune after bloom.

name: Hibiscus
description: National flower of Mauritius. Tropical perennial flowering shrub. Attracts butterflies.
care_notes: Regular watering. Prune lightly after each bloom cycle. Fertilize monthly in growing season.

name: Taro (Dasheen)
description: Traditional Mauritian staple (songe). Grown in wet, low-lying areas. Both leaves and tubers used.
care_notes: Needs consistently moist soil. Can tolerate partial flooding. Harvest corms when leaves yellow.

name: Carrot
description: Root vegetable best grown in Mauritius winter in loose, deep soil.
care_notes: Needs loose, deep, stone-free soil. Thin seedlings to prevent forking. Don't over-fertilize.

name: Onion
description: Essential kitchen vegetable grown in Mauritius winter. Long growing season.
care_notes: Reduce watering when tops start to brown and fall. Cure harvested bulbs in sun.

name: Garlic
description: Plant individual cloves in winter. Ready to harvest when tops dry. Great pest deterrent.
care_notes: Plant pointed end up, 5cm deep. Stop watering when leaves start yellowing. Cure in dry shade.
